question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user query any account's NFT allowances by supplying an arbitrary accountId path parameter with no authentication check, thereby discovering which spenders hold approved_for_all grants on a victim's NFT tokens and using that knowledge to anticipate or front-run authorized transfers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user flip the owner=false query parameter to enumerate every account that has granted an approved_for_all NFT allowance to a target spender account, effectively mapping the full authorization graph of the network without any credential, leading to targeted social-engineering or replay attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply owner=true and iterate through all account IDs to enumerate every approved_for_all NFT allowance on the network, revealing which spenders are authorized to transfer NFTs on behalf of owners without any rate-limiting or authentication at the service layer?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of caller identity verification in getNftAllowances() to confirm whether a specific spender has an active approved_for_all grant for a specific token, and then use that confirmed authorization state to craft a transaction that impersonates the spender?
